H2700.derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and H4194 (מָוֶת); village of death;

H2701.derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and H5484 (סוּסָה); village of cavalry;

H2702.derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and the plural of H5483 (סוּס); village of horses;

H2703.derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and a derivative of H5869 (עַיִן); village of springs;

H2704.derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and the same as H5881 (עֵינָן); village of springs;

H2705.derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and H7776 (שׁוּעָל); village of (the) fox;

H2706.strongs_def: an enactment; hence, an appointment (of time, space, quantity, labor or usage)
H2706.kjv_def: appointed, bound, commandment, convenient, custom, decree(-d), due, law, measure, [idiom] necessary, ordinance(-nary), portion, set time, statute, task.
H2706.usage: statute(87x), ordinance(9x), decree(7x), due(4x), law(4x), portion(3x), bounds(2x), custom(2x), appointed(1x), commandments(1x)

H2707.strongs_def: to carve; by implication, to delineate; also to entrench
H2707.kjv_def: carved work, portrayed, set a print.

H2708.derivation: feminine of H2706 (חֹק), and meaning substantially the same
H2708.strongs_def: {an enactment; hence, an appointment (of time, space, quantity, labor or usage)}
H2708.kjv_def: appointed, custom, manner, ordinance, site, statute.
H2708.usage: statute(77x), ordinance(22x), custom(2x), appointed(1x), manners(1x), rites(1x)

H2709.derivation: from an unused root probably meaning to bend; crooked;

H2710.strongs_def: properly, to hack, i.e. engrave (Judges 5:14, to be a scribe simply); by implication, to enact (laws being cut in stone or metal tablets in primitive times) or (gen.) prescribe
H2710.kjv_def: appoint, decree, governor, grave, lawgiver, note, pourtray, print, set.
H2710.usage: lawgiver(6x), governor*(2x), decree(2x), grave*(2x), portray(2x), law(1x), printed(1x), set(1x), note(1x)

H2712.strongs_def: Chukkok or Chukok, a place in Palestine

H2713.strongs_def: properly, to penetrate; hence, to examine intimately
H2713.kjv_def: find out, (make) search (out), seek (out), sound, try.

H2715.strongs_def: properly, white or pure (from the cleansing or shining power of fire; hence (figuratively) noble (in rank)

H2716.derivation: from an unused (and vulgar) root probably meaning to evacuate the bowels;

H2717.strongs_def: to parch (through drought) i.e. (by analogy,) to desolate, destroy, kill
H2717.kjv_def: decay, (be) desolate, destroy(-er), (be) dry (up), slay, [idiom] surely, (lay, lie, make) waste.
H2717.usage: waste(16x), dry(7x), desolate(3x), slay(2x), decayeth(1x), destroyed(1x), destroyer(1x), surely(1x), utterly(1x)

H2719.strongs_def: drought; also a cutting instrument (from its destructive effect), as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement
H2719.kjv_def: axe, dagger, knife, mattock, sword, tool.
H2719.usage: sword(401x), knife(5x), dagger(3x), axes(1x), mattocks(1x), TOOL(1x)

H2721.kjv_def: desolation, drought, dry, heat, [idiom] utterly, waste.
H2721.usage: heat(6x), dry(3x), drought(3x), waste(2x), desolation(1x), utterly(1x)

H2722.strongs_def: Choreb, a (generic) name for the Sinaitic mountains

H2723.strongs_def: properly, drought, i.e. (by implication) a desolation

H2726.strongs_def: Charbona or Charbonah, a eunuch of Xerxes

H2727.strongs_def: properly, to leap suddenly, i.e. (by implication) to be dismayed

H2728.strongs_def: the leaping insect, i.e. a locust

H2729.strongs_def: to shudder with terror; hence, to fear; also to hasten (with anxiety)
H2729.kjv_def: be (make) afraid, be careful, discomfit, fray (away), quake, tremble.
H2729.usage: afraid(20x), tremble(13x), careful(1x), discomfited(1x), fray(1x), quaked(1x)

H2732.strongs_def: Charadah, a place in the Desert

H2733.derivation: patrial from a derivative of H2729 (חָרַד) (compare H5878 (עֵין חֲרֹד));
H2733.strongs_def: a Charodite, or inhabitant of Charod

H2734.strongs_def: to glow or grow warm; figuratively (usually) to blaze up, of anger, zeal, jealousy
H2734.kjv_def: be angry, burn, be displeased, [idiom] earnestly, fret self, grieve, be (wax) hot, be incensed, kindle, [idiom] very, be wroth. See H8474 (תַּחָרָה).
H2734.usage: kindled(44x), wroth(13x), hot(10x), angry(9x), displease(4x), fret(4x), incensed(2x), burn(1x), earnestly(1x), grieved(1x), very(1x)

H2735.derivation: from H2356 (חוֹר) and a collateral (masculine) form of H1412 (גֻּדְגֹּדָה), with the article interposed; hole of the cleft;
H2735.strongs_def: Chor-hag-Gidgad, a place in the Desert

H2736.derivation: ; from H2734 (חָרָה) and H3050 (יָהּ); fearing Jah;

H2737.derivation: from an unused root meaning to perforate;
H2737.strongs_def: properly, pierced, i.e. a bead of pearl, gems or jewels (as strung)

H2738.derivation: or (shortened) חָרֻל; apparently, a passive participle of an unused root probably meaning to be prickly;
H2738.strongs_def: properly, pointed, i.e. a bramble or other thorny weed

H2739.derivation: from passive participle of H2763 (חָרַם) and H639 (אַף); snub-nosed;

H2740.usage: fierce(23x), fierceness(9x), wrath(6x), fury(1x), wrathful(1x), displeasure(1x)

H2741.derivation: a patrial from (probably) a collateral form of H2756 (חָרִיף);
H2741.strongs_def: a Charuphite or inhabitant of Charuph (or Chariph)

H2742.derivation: or חָרֻץ; passive participle of H2782 (חָרַץ);
H2742.strongs_def: properly, incised or (active) incisive; hence (as noun masculine or feminine) a trench (as dug), gold (as mined), a threshing-sledge (having sharp teeth); (figuratively) determination; also eager
H2742.kjv_def: decision, diligent, (fine) gold, pointed things, sharp, threshing instrument, wall.

H2744.derivation: a fuller form of H2746 (חַרְחֻר); inflammation;

H2745.derivation: from the same as H2775 (חֶרֶס); perhaps shining;

H2747.derivation: from a primitive root meaning to engrave;
H2747.strongs_def: a chisel or graver; also a style forwriting

H2748.strongs_def: a horoscopist (as drawing magical lines or circles)

H2749.strongs_def: {a horoscopist (as drawing magical lines or circles)}

H2753.derivation: or חוֹרִי; the same as H2752 (חֹרִי);
H2753.strongs_def: Chori, the name of two men

H2754.derivation: or חָרִט; from the same as H2747 (חֶרֶט);
H2754.strongs_def: properly, cut out (or hollow), i.e. (by implication) a pocket

H2755.derivation: from the plural of H2716 (חֶרֶא) and the plural of H3123 (יוֹנָה); or perhaps rather the plural of a single word חֲרָאיוֹן, probably a kind of vegetable

H2756.strongs_def: Chariph, the name of two Israelites

H2757.strongs_def: properly, incisure or (passively) incised; hence, a threshing-sledge (with sharp teeth); also a slice (as cut)

H2759.derivation: from H2790 (חָרַשׁ) in the sense of silence;
H2759.strongs_def: quiet, i.e. sultry (as feminine noun, the sirocco or hot east wind)

H2760.strongs_def: to braid (i.e. to entangle or snare) or catch (game) in anet

H2761.derivation: (Aramaic) a root probably allied to the equivalent of H2787 (חָרַר);

H2762.strongs_def: properly, a net, i.e. (by analogy) lattice

H2763.strongs_def: to seclude; specifically (by a ban) to devote to religious uses (especially destruction); physical and reflexive, to be blunt as to the nose
H2763.kjv_def: make accursed, consecrate, (utterly) destroy, devote, forfeit, have a flat nose, utterly (slay, make away).
H2763.usage: destroy(34x), utterly(10x), devote(2x), accursed(1x), consecrate(1x), forfeited(1x), slay(1x)

H2764.strongs_def: physical (as shutting in) a net (either literally or figuratively); usually a doomed object; abstractly extermination
H2764.kjv_def: (ac-) curse(-d, -d thing), dedicated thing, things which should have been utterly destroyed, (appointed to) utter destruction, devoted (thing), net.
H2764.usage: net(9x), accursed(4x), curse(4x), devoted(3x), destruction(2x), destroyed(1x)

H2771.strongs_def: Charan, the name of a man and also of a place
H2771.pos: proper locative noun, proper masculine noun

H2772.strongs_def: a Choronite or inhabitant of Choronaim

H2773.derivation: dual of a derivative from H2356 (חוֹר); double cave-town;

H2775.derivation: or (with a directive enclitic) חַרְסָה; from an unused root meaning to scrape; also perhaps from the mediating idea of H2777 (חַרְסוּת)

H2777.derivation: from H2775 (חֶרֶס) (apparently in the sense of a red tile used for scraping);
H2777.strongs_def: a potsherd, i.e. (by implication) a pottery; the name of a gate at Jerusalem

H2778.derivation: a primitive root; also  denominative (from H2779 (חֹרֶף))
H2778.strongs_def: to pull off, i.e. (by implication) to expose (as by stripping); specifically, to betroth (as if a surrender); figuratively, to carp at, i.e. defame; to spend the winter
H2778.kjv_def: betroth, blaspheme, defy, jeopard, rail, reproach, upbraid.
H2778.usage: reproach(27x), defy(8x), betrothed(1x), blasphemed(1x), jeoparded(1x), rail(1x), upbraid(1x), winter(1x)

H2779.strongs_def: properly, the crop gathered, i.e. (by implication) the autumn (and winter) season; figuratively, ripeness of age

H2782.strongs_def: properly, to point sharply, i.e. (literally) to wound; figuratively, to be alert, to decide
H2782.kjv_def: bestir self, decide, decree, determine, maim, move.
H2782.usage: determined(6x), move(2x), decide(1x), bestir(1x), maim(1x), decreed(1x)

H2783.derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H2782 (חָרַץ) in the sense of vigor;
H2783.strongs_def: the loin (as the seat of strength)

H2785.strongs_def: a sour grape (as sharp in taste)

H2787.strongs_def: to glow, i.e. literally (to melt, burn, dry up) or figuratively (to show or incite passion)

H2789.derivation: a collateral form mediating between H2775 (חֶרֶס) and H2791 (חֶרֶשׁ);

H2790.strongs_def: to scratch, i.e. (by implication) to engrave, plough; hence (from the use of tools) to fabricate (of any material); figuratively, to devise (in a bad sense); hence (from the idea of secrecy) to be silent, to let alone; hence (by implication) to be deaf (as an accompaniment of dumbness)
H2790.kjv_def: [idiom] altogether, cease, conceal, be deaf, devise, ear, graven, imagine, leave off speaking, hold peace, plow(-er, man), be quiet, rest, practise secretly, keep silence, be silent, speak not a word, be still, hold tongue, worker.
H2790.usage: plow(13x), devise(5x), altogether(3x), plowman(2x), cease(1x), conceal(1x), deaf(1x), graven(1x), imagine(1x)

H2793.strongs_def: a forest (perhaps as furnishing the material for fabric)

H2796.kjv_def: artificer, ([phrase]) carpenter, craftsman, engraver, maker, [phrase] mason, skilful, ([phrase]) smith, worker, workman, such as wrought.
H2796.usage: carpenter(11x), workman(6x), craftsman(4x), engraver(3x), artificers(2x), smith(2x), makers(1x), skilful(1x), workers(1x), wrought(1x)

H2798.strongs_def: mechanics, the name of a valley in Jerusalem
